meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve Project Bloat
content.seo_description: Learn how the Antigravity project template overcomes the 'can't finish projects' hurdle. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' syndrome stems from architectural paralysis and scope creep. By using the Antigravity project template with Claude Code, you enforce a modular, constraint-based development workflow. This system automates boilerplate setup, forces MVP scoping, and provides a clear path from local environment to deployment in minutes.
content.intro:
You sit down at 8 PM, fueled by ambition and a fresh GitHub repo, ready to build the next big thing. By 11 PM, you’re drowning in dependency errors, lost in a labyrinth of file structures, and the initial spark of genius has been extinguished by the cold reality of 'tutorial hell.' You aren't failing because you lack talent; you’re failing because you lack a system.

The Antigravity project template is the antidote to the unfinished graveyard of projects on your hard drive. It provides the skeletal structure and automated scaffolding necessary to move from idea to production-ready code without the friction that usually kills your momentum. It’s time to stop starting and start finishing.

steps.title: Bootstrap with Antigravity
steps.description: Initialize your project environment using the Antigravity CLI to ensure all essential dependencies and CI/CD pipelines are pre-configured.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS starter project with authentication, database integration, and a responsive UI layout using the Antigravity base template.

steps.title: Define MVP Scope with Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to parse your project requirements and strip away non-essential features, ensuring you focus on the core value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project goals and define the absolute minimum features required for a functional MVP. Create a checklist.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project requirements and suggest a scope-reduced MVP list.'

steps.title: Automated File Scaffolding
steps.description: Let the AI generate the necessary file structure based on your selected stack, preventing the dreaded 'blank page' syndrome.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the folder structure and boilerplate files for a Next.js application following the Antigravity modular architecture pattern.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate project structure based on Antigravity standards'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Feed your business logic into Claude Code, allowing it to write clean, maintainable code within the established project template.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the user authentication flow and database schema using Prisma, adhering to the project's existing coding standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement auth flow using Prisma and NextAuth'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Use the built-in deployment scripts to push your code to a staging environment, turning your local project into a live URL.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the deployment pipeline script to verify all environment variables and build steps for production deployment.

pro_tips: Treat your project like a product, not code; if a feature doesn't solve the core problem, delete it immediately.
Use Claude Code's 'context' mode to keep track of your project's architectural decisions so you don't repeat mistakes.
Always commit after every successful feature implementation, even if the code is messy; progress is a psychological win.
Focus on the 'Happy Path' first; ignore edge cases until the core functionality is fully deployed and tested.
Use Antigravity's environment variable templates to avoid hard-coding secrets—security is a habit, not an afterthought.
resources: Best Practices for SaaS Architecture: https://github.com/saas-builders/guide
content.next_steps: Now that your MVP is live, identify one user to test it. Gather feedback, iterate on the core feature, and avoid adding new features for at least two weeks to master the 'finish' mindset.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the template, and the roadmap. The only thing missing is your commitment to shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
